content:
This war makes Israel weaker

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

The Cover page of a book: The The Six-Day War Six-Day War: The Breaking of the Middle East by Guy LaronIn 1967, Israel fought a war on three fronts — Egypt, Jordan, and Syria — and won in six days. It was the most decisive military victory of the 20th century. The entire Israeli doctrine was born from that template: hit first, hit hard, end it fast. Go back to work on Sunday.
That model worked for sixty years. Every war Israel fought — 1967, 1973, 1982, 2006, 2014 — lasted weeks or months, never years. The economy could absorb the shock. Reservists could return to their jobs. The country could reset.
But since October 7, 2023, Israel has been fighting continuously. Gaza. Then Lebanon. Then Iran.
Nearly three years of sustained combat across multiple fronts. And the country that was engineered for short, sharp wars is now discovering what happens when the war doesn’t end.
